digest: Standard-depth audited handoff: practical RAG evaluation should combine retrieval diagnostics, groundedness/answer checks, calibrated LLM judges, curated offline tests, online trace monitoring, and privacy/security controls; 13 claims verified, 3 partial, no unsupported/conflicting.

finalReport.summary: Working conclusion: for a small SaaS team, the best-supported production RAG quality program is layered rather than single-metric: keep retrieval metrics for root-cause diagnosis, evaluate groundedness/answer quality separately, use LLM-as-judge only with rubrics and human calibration, maintain curated regression datasets, monitor production traces and drift, and treat telemetry as sensitive data. The small-team stack recommendation is a practical synthesis, not a directly vendor-mandated pattern.

researchMetadata.sourcePolicy: Primary/official docs and recognized security guidance preferred; audit-claims verifier verdicts are authoritative; partial claims remain caveated rather than promoted.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Retrieval metrics cover coverage/noise/ranking; context precision/recall, MAP, nDCG, and MRR have support, but exact recall@k/hit-rate/MRR formulas need stronger primary verification.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: High: metric definitions anchor the evaluation brief.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Retrieval-only metrics diagnose retriever/context quality but do not guarantee final answer quality.

Groundedness can be checked by claim-level support against retrieved context; citation validation is stronger when paired with context/ranking checks, but the combined prescription is partial synthesis.
LLM judges can be useful but are biased and setup-dependent; use explicit rubrics and human corrections/few-shot calibration.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: High: judge scores may drive gates and alerts.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Online evaluation of live interactions complements offline datasets; human review is useful for calibration and risky/escalated cases, but sampling rates/cadences are not standardized here.
Maintain stable curated datasets for repeatable offline regression tests before shipping.
Synthetic tests can broaden coverage via knowledge graphs, personas/scenarios, and query synthesis; do not treat them as a replacement for real/golden data without local validation.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Medium: improves coverage but can misrepresent production.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Monitor trace-derived quality/performance metrics and distribution drift against baselines; RAG-specific drift signals are synthesized from general docs.
RAG telemetry can expose prompts, retrieved docs, outputs, and metadata; evaluate indirect prompt injection, poisoning, and exfiltration risks; use masking/minimization.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Critical: unsafe telemetry can create security/privacy exposure.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Ragas/TruLens/LangSmith/Phoenix/DeepEval cover overlapping eval/observability capabilities; current-doc validation is needed before procurement or ranking.
A feasible small-team pattern is offline metric/regression gates plus always-on tracing/operational metrics, sampled quality evals, and privacy controls; feasibility/sampling are synthesized.
Use trace dashboards, aggregate quality/performance metrics, drift alerts, and CI/release gates on curated datasets; exact alert/review cadence is local policy.

Handle uncertainty by treating judge/faithfulness scores as calibrated signals, using rubrics, human corrections, and escalation for low/conflicting/risky outputs; no universal numeric thresholds were verified.

mainFindings.finding: Retrieval metrics are necessary diagnostics but not final RAG quality guarantees.
mainFindings.implication: Separate retriever debugging from final-answer acceptance.

mainFindings.finding: Groundedness/faithfulness can be evaluated at claim level against retrieved context.
mainFindings.implication: Use claim support rate as a core hallucination signal.

mainFindings.finding: LLM-as-judge is scalable but must be calibrated and bias-aware.
mainFindings.implication: Do not use judge output as unreviewed ground truth for critical decisions.

mainFindings.finding: Curated offline datasets plus online production evaluation are complementary.
mainFindings.implication: Use offline regression gates before release and online signals after release.

mainFindings.finding: RAG observability introduces privacy/security risk because traces can contain sensitive inputs, retrieved content, outputs, and metadata.
mainFindings.implication: Logging design and masking are part of the quality program, not optional add-ons.

recommendations.recommendation: Adopt a layered eval design: retriever metrics, answer groundedness/relevance, calibrated LLM judge checks, and operational traces should be reported separately.
recommendations.caveat: The exact dashboard grouping is implementation guidance; the underlying metric separation is verified.

recommendations.recommendation: Make curated offline regression datasets the release gate, then use online trace evaluation for production issue detection.
evidence: LangSmith evaluation concepts and dataset docs
recommendations.caveat: No universal pass/fail thresholds were verified; set thresholds from local baseline behavior.

recommendations.recommendation: Use LLM-as-judge only with explicit rubrics, human corrections/few-shot calibration, and periodic audit sampling.
recommendations.caveat: Judge reliability is task- and setup-dependent.

recommendations.recommendation: For a small team, start with local/open eval metrics plus hosted or self-hosted tracing, but validate vendor capabilities immediately before choosing a tool.
evidence: Ragas, DeepEval, LangSmith, Phoenix, TruLens official docs
recommendations.caveat: Small-team feasibility and sampling strategy are synthesized, not directly established by a single source.

recommendations.recommendation: Minimize and mask telemetry before sending it to hosted observability backends; include prompt-injection/RAG-poisoning/exfiltration cases in security evaluation.
evidence: OWASP LLM01 and Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
recommendations.caveat: Specific masking rules must be adapted to the team’s data classes and compliance needs.

actionPlan.step: Define metric taxonomy and dashboard sections: retrieval, groundedness/answer quality, judge confidence/caveats, operational latency/cost, and security/privacy events.

Create a small curated regression dataset from known customer intents and failure modes; include inputs, expected/reference outputs where useful, retrieved-context expectations, and security/adversarial cases.
Implement claim-level faithfulness checks and context precision/recall/ranking diagnostics in CI or pre-release experiments.
Add production tracing with masking/minimization, then sample traces into online eval and human review queues.
Run a short tool bake-off using current docs for Ragas, DeepEval, LangSmith, Phoenix, and TruLens; compare privacy controls, self-hosting/hosted workflow, CI fit, tracing integration, and cost.
sources: Official tool docs listed in slot-010

caveatedFindings.finding: Production RAG should track retrieval metrics separately from answer metrics.
caveatedFindings.caveat: Sources support the metric taxonomy and separate RAG dimensions; the production-specific 'should track separately' wording is best-practice synthesis.

caveatedFindings.finding: Citation/source validation should combine answer support checks with retrieved-context usefulness/ranking checks.
caveatedFindings.caveat: Docs support separate faithfulness and context precision metrics; no inspected source mandated this exact combined citation-validation method.

caveatedFindings.finding: Small SaaS teams should use offline/local eval libraries plus hosted/self-hosted tracing and sampled quality evals.
caveatedFindings.caveat: Components are supported by official docs; feasibility and sampling policy require local validation.

unverifiedButRelevant.claim: ALCE defines automatic metrics for fluency, correctness, and citation quality for cited-answer systems.
Production traces can be collected into datasets and reused downstream for testing.
unverifiedButRelevant.whyItMatters: Useful lifecycle loop from production to regression data.

unverifiedButRelevant.claim: CI-friendly evaluation frameworks can make minimal RAG eval feasible for small teams.
Small teams should prefer minimization, redaction, least privilege, output validation, and human review for risky cases over unmanaged comprehensive telemetry.
unverifiedButRelevant.whyItMatters: Useful security posture, already partly covered by verified security claims.

unverifiedButRelevant.claim: DeepEval predefined metrics are largely LLM-as-judge metrics returning 0–1 scores with reasoning.
unverifiedButRelevant.whyItMatters: Tool-specific detail for a comparison table.

unverifiedButRelevant.claim: Phoenix eval workflows can run built-in templates on trace-derived data and log structured quality signals back to Phoenix.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Choose whether the immediate deliverable is an evaluation architecture or a vendor/tool recommendation.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: The evidence strongly supports architectural layers; tool ranking is only high-level and time-sensitive.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: If selecting tools, run a current-doc bake-off before committing.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Do not set numeric thresholds for pass/fail, drift, abstention, or sampling from this research alone.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: No universal numeric thresholds were verified; using invented thresholds would create false precision.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Derive thresholds from baseline data and human-reviewed calibration samples.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Decide acceptable telemetry exposure before enabling production tracing.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: Quality monitoring can log sensitive prompts, retrieved docs, outputs, and metadata.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Require masking/minimization and access controls as launch criteria.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Treat synthetic tests as coverage expansion, not as a substitute for real/golden cases.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: Synthetic generation methods are sourced, but representativeness caveats are thin.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Blend synthetic/adversarial cases with curated production-derived cases.

blocking.gap: Current vendor/tool capability validation before procurement or final stack ranking.
blocking.whyItMatters: Tool docs change quickly and the audited comparison is high-level, not an exhaustive matrix.

blocking.gap: Local thresholds for release gates, alerts, abstention, and sampling.
blocking.whyItMatters: No universal numeric thresholds were verified; thresholds affect false positives, cost, and safety.

nonBlockingCaveats.gap: Exact formulas for recall@k, hit rate, and some ranking measures need stronger primary verification before publishing formula tables.
nonBlockingCaveats.whyItMatters: Does not block architecture, but matters for implementation docs.

nonBlockingCaveats.gap: Synthetic data caveats need stronger independent evidence.
nonBlockingCaveats.whyItMatters: Affects how much weight synthetic tests should receive.

nonBlockingCaveats.gap: RAG-specific drift signals such as corpus/query/retrieval/feedback drift are synthesized from general monitoring/drift docs.
nonBlockingCaveats.whyItMatters: Use cautious wording and validate against observed production signals.

claims.support: Metric taxonomy supported; production separation is inferred.
claims.caveat: Use narrower wording for hard claims.
claims.correctionOrCounterclaim: RAG retrieval evaluation can include context recall/precision and MAP/nDCG/MRR; separate tracking is best-practice synthesis.

claims.support: Retrieval metrics are distinct from generation faithfulness/relevance dimensions.
claims.caveat: Citation correctness/safety are inferred outside retrieval scope.

claims.support: Ragas directly describes claim extraction, support checking, and supported/total formula.
claims.caveat: Verifies Ragas faithfulness metric, not all groundedness methods.

claims.support: Separate faithfulness and context precision checks are supported.
claims.correctionOrCounterclaim: Pairing answer support with retrieval/context checks can strengthen citation validation; not a mandated procedure in inspected docs.

claims.support: Strong LLM judges approximate human preferences in benchmark settings with limitations.
Position, verbosity, self-enhancement biases and reasoning limitations are directly identified.
LangSmith docs support rubrics/scoring criteria plus human corrections/few-shot calibration.
Offline curated datasets and online production evaluation are explicitly distinguished and complementary.
Curated datasets enable repeatable pre-shipping comparisons/regression tests.
claims.caveat: Docs say datasets/curated datasets rather than exact phrase golden datasets.
Persona availability may vary by version.

claims.support: Traces can be aggregated into performance/quality metrics, dashboards, and alerts.
claims.caveat: Exact retention wording is inferred from trace data docs.

claims.support: Drift compares reference/current distributions and can alert on configured drifting-signal share.
claims.caveat: Built-in alerting documented as Pro/Cloud/Enterprise feature.

claims.support: Traces may include sensitive data and retrieved documents; client-side masking can reduce hosted-backend exposure.
OWASP supports indirect prompt injection, RAG poisoning, and exfiltration risk cases.
claims.caveat: Security eval inclusion is supported by testing/monitoring guidance rather than one exact sentence.

claims.support: Official docs support high-level tool capability comparison.
claims.caveat: Not exhaustive and should be refreshed before vendor decisions.

claims.support: Local metrics and hosted/self-hosted tracing components are supported.
claims.caveat: Small-SaaS feasibility and sampled judging policy are synthesis.
claims.correctionOrCounterclaim: Combine local metric evals with observability/tracing; justify sampling separately.

claims.caveat: Core security facts verified elsewhere; this exact recommendation was not adjudicated.
